stem: In triangle ABD, point C lies on segment BD such that AC is perpendicular to BD. If angle ABD = 30 degrees, angle ADB = 45 degrees, and AD = 10, what is the length of BD?
explanation: The perpendicular AC creates two right triangles: triangle ACD (right angle at C) and triangle ACB (right angle at C). In right triangle ACD: angle D = 45 degrees and hypotenuse AD = 10, so AC = 10 sin(45) = 5sqrt(2) and CD = 10 cos(45) = 5sqrt(2). In right triangle ACB: angle B = 30 degrees and AC = 5sqrt(2). Since tan(30) = AC/BC, we get BC = AC/tan(30) = 5sqrt(2) / (1/sqrt(3)) = 5sqrt(2) * sqrt(3) = 5sqrt(6). Therefore BD = BC + CD = 5sqrt(6) + 5sqrt(2) = 5(sqrt(6) + sqrt(2)). Verification: In triangle ACB, tan(30) = 5sqrt(2) / (5sqrt(6)) = sqrt(2)/sqrt(6) = 1/sqrt(3), which equals tan(30). Check confirmed.

distractorRationale.A: Swaps the two given angles, applying 45 degrees to triangle ACB and 30 degrees to triangle ACD. In triangle ACD with angle D = 30: AC = 10 sin(30) = 5 and CD = 10 cos(30) = 5sqrt(3). In triangle ACB with angle B = 45: BC = AC/tan(45) = 5. Then BD = BC + CD = 5 + 5sqrt(3) = 5(1 + \sqrt{3})
distractorRationale.B: Correctly computes AC = 5sqrt(2) and CD = 5sqrt(2) in triangle ACD, and correctly finds BC = 5sqrt(6) in triangle ACB, but reports only BC as the answer, forgetting to add CD to get the full length BD = 5\sqrt{6}
distractorRationale.C: Uses AD = 10 as a leg of the 45-45-90 triangle rather than the hypotenuse, giving AC = 10 and CD = 10. Then in triangle ACB: BC = 10/tan(30) = 10sqrt(3). So BD = 10sqrt(3) + 10 = 10(\sqrt{3} + 1)
distractorRationale.D: Correct: Finds AC = CD = 5sqrt(2) from the 45-degree right triangle, then BC = 5sqrt(6) from the 30-degree right triangle, and computes BD = 5sqrt(6) + 5sqrt(2) = 5(\sqrt{6} + \sqrt{2})

imageDescription: Triangle ABD is shown with vertices labeled. Point C lies on side BD between B and D. A dashed line from A to C is drawn perpendicular to BD, with a small square marking the right angle at C. Angle ABD (at vertex B) is labeled 30 degrees, angle ADB (at vertex D) is labeled 45 degrees, and side AD is labeled 10. The segments BC and CD are marked along the base BD.
